questions.question: How many spending records have a total spending in 2023 greater than 1,000,000?
questions.reasoning: This query counts SpendingRecord entities. It filters these entities based on the 'total_spending_2023' attribute, selecting only those where the value is greater than 1,000,000. This fits the T2 criteria of a single entity type and a numeric filter.

questions.question: What is the average total dosage units for spending records where the average spend per unit in 2023 is less than 5?
questions.reasoning: This query calculates the average of 'total_dosage_units_2023' for SpendingRecord entities. The filter condition checks if 'avg_spend_per_unit_2023' is less than 5. This adheres to T2 requirements: one entity type, a numeric filter, and an aggregation.

questions.question: Find the total number of spending records where the average spend per claim in 2023 contains '3' in its string representation.
questions.reasoning: This query counts SpendingRecord entities. The filter uses `CONTAINS(STR(?avgSpendPerClaim), "3")` to check for the presence of the digit '3' in the string representation of 'avg_spend_per_claim_2023'. This satisfies the T2 criteria of a single entity type and a string-based filter.

questions.question: What is the maximum total claims in 2023 for spending records that have '802020' as their total spending in 2023?
questions.reasoning: This query finds the maximum 'total_claims_2023' for SpendingRecord entities. The filter condition `?totalSpending = "802020"` uses an exact string match on the 'total_spending_2023' attribute. This adheres to T2 requirements: one entity type, an equality filter, and an aggregation.

questions.question: Which drugs have spending records with a total spending of more than 1,000,000 in 2023, and how many such records does each drug have?
questions.gold_sparql:
SELECT ?drugLabel (COUNT(?spendingRecord) AS ?numberOfSpendingRecords) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing> WHERE {
  ?drug <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/Drug> .
  ?drug <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> ?drugLabel .
  ?drug <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/has_spending_record> ?spendingRecord .
  ?spendingRecord <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/SpendingRecord> .
  ?spendingRecord <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/total_spending_2023> ?totalSpending .
  FILTER (xsd:float(?totalSpending) > 1000000) .
} GROUP BY ?drugLabel ORDER BY DESC(?numberOfSpendingRecords)
questions.tier_classifier_reasoning: 2 distinct entity types connected by 1 predicate
questions.expected_answer:
Potassium Chloride* | 21
Gabapentin | 18
Fenofibrate | 18
Atorvastatin Calcium | 17
Diclofenac Sodium* | 16
Doxepin HCl* | 15
Metoprolol Succinate | 15
Baclofen* | 15
Clobetasol Propionate | 15
Famotidine* | 14
... (2022 total rows)
questions.reasoning: This query joins Drug and SpendingRecord entities. It filters SpendingRecord by 'total_spending_2023' being greater than 1,000,000 and then groups by drug label to count the number of such spending records per drug. This satisfies the two-entity join requirement with a filter on one side and a GROUP BY aggregation.

questions.question: For each manufacturer, how many spending records are associated with them where the average spend per claim in 2023 was greater than 100?
questions.gold_sparql:
SELECT ?manufacturerName (COUNT(?spendingRecord) AS ?numberOfSpendingRecords) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing> WHERE {
  ?manufacturer <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/Manufacturer> .
  ?manufacturer <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/manufacturer_name> ?manufacturerName .
  ?manufacturer <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/manufacturer_has_spending> ?spendingRecord .
  ?spendingRecord <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/SpendingRecord> .
  ?spendingRecord <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/avg_spend_per_claim_2023> ?avgSpendPerClaim .
  FILTER (xsd:float(?avgSpendPerClaim) > 100) .
} GROUP BY ?manufacturerName ORDER BY DESC(?numberOfSpendingRecords)
questions.tier_classifier_reasoning: 2 distinct entity types connected by 1 predicate

Aurobindo Pharm | 73
Lupin Pharmaceu | 69

... (736 total rows)
questions.reasoning: This query connects Manufacturer and SpendingRecord entities. It filters SpendingRecord by 'avg_spend_per_claim_2023' being greater than 100 and then groups by manufacturer name to count the associated spending records. This fulfills the two-entity join with a filter and aggregation.

questions.question: List the generic drugs and the number of brand drugs associated with each, where the brand drug's total manufacturers count is greater than 1.
questions.gold_sparql:
SELECT ?genericName (COUNT(DISTINCT ?brandDrug) AS ?numberOfBrandDrugs) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing> WHERE {
  ?genericDrug <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/GenericDrug> .
  ?genericDrug <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/generic_name> ?genericName .
  ?brandDrug <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/Drug> .
  ?brandDrug <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/has_generic> ?genericDrug .
  ?brandDrug <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/total_manufacturers> ?totalManufacturers .
  FILTER (xsd:integer(?totalManufacturers) > 1) .
} GROUP BY ?genericName ORDER BY DESC(?numberOfBrandDrugs)
questions.tier_classifier_reasoning: 2 distinct entity types connected by 1 predicate
questions.expected_answer:
Pen Needle, Diabetic | 10
Budesonide | 6
Diltiazem HCl | 6
Methylphenidate HCl | 5
Estradiol | 4
Metformin HCl | 4
Alcohol Antiseptic Pads | 4
Verapamil HCl | 3
Bupropion HCl | 3
Mesalamine | 3
... (910 total rows)
questions.reasoning: This query links GenericDrug and Drug (brand drug) entities. It filters the brand drug by 'total_manufacturers' being greater than 1 and then groups by generic drug name to count the distinct brand drugs. This adheres to the two-entity join with a filter on one side and a GROUP BY aggregation.

questions.question: For each manufacturer, what is the total number of distinct drugs they produce that have an associated spending record with a total spending in 2023 greater than 100000?
questions.gold_sparql:
SELECT ?manufacturerName (COUNT(DISTINCT ?drug) AS ?numberOfDrugs) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing> WHERE {
  ?manufacturer <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/Manufacturer> .
  ?manufacturer <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/manufacturer_name> ?manufacturerName .
  ?manufacturer <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/manufacturer_has_spending> ?spendingRecord .
  ?spendingRecord <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/SpendingRecord> .
  ?spendingRecord <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/spending_for_drug> ?drug .
  ?drug <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/type/Drug> .
  ?spendingRecord <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-medicare-part-d-pricing/pred/total_spending_2023> ?totalSpending .
  FILTER(xsd:float(?totalSpending) > 100000) .
} GROUP BY ?manufacturerName
questions.expected_answer:
Somerset Therap | 12
Oceanside Pharm | 41
Fosun Pharma Us | 6
Apotex Corp* | 5
Thea Pharma Inc | 6
Sun Pharma Glob* | 1
Ranbaxy/Sun Pha | 8
Westminster Pha | 27
Bionpharma Inc. | 34
Mylan/Archis Ph | 4
... (736 total rows)
questions.reasoning: This query uses Option A. It involves three distinct entity types: Manufacturer, SpendingRecord, and Drug. The relationships form a path: Manufacturer -> SpendingRecord -> Drug. It aggregates the count of distinct drugs per manufacturer, filtered by a spending record attribute. All entity variables have rdf:type triples.
